channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Top agricultural bodies meet to boost reforms and strengthen sector
item.description: In Dodoma, the Agriculture Minister, Daniel Chongolo, gathered top executives from major agricultural institutions to push forward reforms and strengthen agriculture's role in Tanzania's economy. Dire...

item.title: Judge Grants Conditional Discharge to Avoid Deportation for First-Time Offender in New Brunswick
item.description: Adebowale Adekoya, a 33-year-old Nigerian man, was originally sentenced to a year of probation after breaching a no-contact order with his former partner in New Brunswick. The probation sentence led t...

item.title: AI traffic enforcement drives rise in fines across Australian states
item.description: A new wave of artificial intelligence–powered traffic enforcement cameras is driving a surge in fines across Australia, revealing the disparities between state penalties and enforcement priorities. Ac...

item.title: Key Objectives and Challenges in the Latest U.S.-Iran Negotiations
item.description: The Wall Street Journal’s coverage of 'What to Expect From Today’s U.S.-Iran Talks' focuses on renewed diplomatic efforts amid widespread tension following recent military exchanges involving Iran, Is...

item.title: Islamabad Hosts Critical US-Iran Peace Talks Amid Global Stakes
item.description: Islamabad has taken centre stage in global diplomacy as it hosts the high-stakes US-Iran talks. For the first time in history, all eyes of the world are on Pakistan’s capital. The negotiations aim to ...

item.title: Britain puts Chagos Islands sovereignty deal on hold amid US concerns
item.description: Britain has temporarily halted its plans to hand over sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius following strong opposition from the United States. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government announ...

item.title: Inside Polymarket: How Online Prediction Markets Are Betting on Real-World Conflicts
item.description: An investigation by The Guardian has revealed the inner workings of Polymarket, an online prediction market where users place bets on global events, including wars and political conflicts. Thousands o...

item.title: UK suspends Chagos Islands transfer amid US opposition and concerns for military base
item.description: The British government has paused its plan to cede sovereignty of the Chagos Islands, which host the strategic Diego Garcia air base, following opposition from the United States. Prime Minister Keir S...

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Start in Islamabad; NASA’s Artemis II Successfully Returns from Moon
item.description: High-level peace discussions between the United States and Iran commenced in Islamabad, aiming to negotiate a truce amid ongoing tensions. Iran’s Vice-President Mohammad Reza Aref cautioned that any a...

item.title: Tribal gas stations in the U.S. provide lower fuel prices due to tax exemptions
item.description: Drivers across the United States are finding relief from rising petrol prices by filling up at tribally owned gas stations. Following the escalation of the conflict involving Iran, national fuel price...

item.title: Blessing Nkor Signs for Dynamo Brest While Tolu Arokodare Attracts Interest from Beşiktaş
item.description: Super Falcons forward Blessing Nkor has completed her move to Belarusian Women’s Premier League club Dynamo Brest. The 22-year-old Nigerian striker signed a contract that will run until the end of the...

item.title: Why conviction matters more than knowledge in promoting lasting behaviour change
item.description: This article from Nation Media explores the idea that even though many people in Kenya and elsewhere have access to information about good practices—like proper health habits, gender equality, or envi...

item.title: US and Iran Begin Fragile Peace Talks in Pakistan Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran are holding high-stakes peace talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, after a fragile two-week ceasefire paused a brutal conflict that has shaken the Middle East. US Vice President JD...

item.title: Bangladesh Requests Extradition of Former PM Sheikh Hasina During Official India Visit
item.description: During his visit to New Delhi, Bangladesh’s Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman formally reiterated Dhaka’s demand that India extradite former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. This request stems from the pr...

item.title: Shashi Tharoor criticises Pakistan's role in mediating Iran-US ceasefire talks
item.description: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor has criticised Pakistan for its involvement in mediating talks between Iran and the United States. Speaking to reporters in New Delhi on Saturday, Tharoor highlighted allega...

item.title: Tanzania introduces pre-paid fuel tax system to reduce evasion and improve distribution
item.description: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania – The Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) recently launched a new fuel tax system aimed at tightening revenue collection and preventing tax evasion in the petroleum sector. Under ...

item.title: Ontario’s Advertising Spending Raises Concerns About Partisan Promotion
item.description: In a recent opinion piece by Marcus Gee for The Globe and Mail, the author criticizes the Ontario government under Premier Doug Ford for spending record amounts on self-promotional advertising campaig...

item.title: Person Arrested Following Molotov Cocktail Incident at OpenAI CEO’s House in San Francisco
item.description: In San Francisco, a 20-year-old man was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the residence of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman early on Friday morning. The incident occurred at approximately 4:1...

item.title: LTFRB takes action against TNVS driver following harassment complaint by Xyriel Manabat
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has temporarily suspended and issued a summons to a TNVS (Transport Network Vehicle Service) driver after receiving a harassment compla...

item.title: Using Lunar Impact Flashes to Detect Interstellar Objects Near the Moon
item.description: In his Medium article, Avi Loeb discusses the intriguing possibility of using the Moon itself as a natural detector for interstellar objects. He explains that the dark side of the Moon provides a perf...
